$comment: SMGP team-season history capsules, season 8 (SMGP-024). Base-universe history: the world's own arc, NOT the player career. DISPLAY-ONLY, never a fold input.

team.madonna.summary: The king met the decade's loudest question the way kings do: by refusing to blink. Asked in winter whether the insurgent's new thunder worried him, A. Senna simply looked at his own car, then spent the season being the verdict in G. Ceara's mirrors: Brazil's war drum answered, Japan's boil survived, Monaco nose-to-tail through the tunnel's dark with a breath between them and the Armco. The MADONNA 456 never raised its voice; the PALM 190 V10 never missed. A. Asselin held the door on everyone else. The challenge was real. So was the answer.
team.madonna.objective: Meet the loaded gun without blinking and remain the verdict in its mirrors.
team.madonna.definingEvent: The tunnel duel: nose-to-tail through the dark, a breath from the Armco.
team.madonna.carryForward: The insurgent's thunder is recalled by winter politics; the crown's answer to the decade's question stands.

team.firenze.summary: The house of old duels watched the new one with a connoisseur's eye and a bookkeeper's hands. While the king and the insurgent burned fuel on each other, F. Elssler banked what their fire spilled, and I. Germi's tight-round opera played to grandstands distracted by the main event. The FIRENZE 500 ran mirror-bright as ever, the FIRENZE 99 V12's aria occasionally audible between the war drums. The Australia tribunal was read with particular attention: the house had settled its own arguments for decades, and now the sport had a law for it.
team.firenze.objective: Bank what the titans spill and study the tribunal's precedent like a deed.
team.firenze.definingEvent: The tribunal's precedent, read in the wine-dark garage with a deed-hunter's care.
team.firenze.carryForward: The ice has a new document in its file, and the opera notes the grandstands can be recaptured.

team.millions.summary: The loudest garage in the paddock spent the season being out-louded by a backstreet, and did not enjoy it. The gold freight rolled on, development spends nobody dared publish, but the grandstands had one song and it was not chrome. N. Jones turned his volume to maximum and found the dial went no further; G. Alberti divided his season between the #6 and the veterans' table, his signature beside Moreau's on every safety paper. The DICK MD V10 ran fresh and furious. The MILLIONS 189 was fast, rich, and somehow the third story in every newspaper.
team.millions.objective: Stay louder than the duel, spend what it takes, and keep the patrician's second seat warm.
team.millions.definingEvent: The freight out-drawn: the loudest garage reduced to the third story.
team.millions.carryForward: The volume dial is broken, the invoices are not, and the patrician's other seat grows more comfortable.

team.bestowal.summary: The cathedral ran its organ at full stops and the season's reviewers were watching the fire next door. M. Blume's metronome and A. Picos's warm Sundays produced exactly the campaign the marble garage ordered, the BESTOWAL 167's sealed VAPOR DN answering the works thunder with development spends nobody dared publish, and the power cathedrals stayed Bestowal country. Yet the year's headlines belonged to the king and the insurgent, and the given house felt the slight the way it feels everything: coldly, and at length. The benefit was real, though: nobody was watching the cathedral re-arm for next spring.
team.bestowal.objective: Hold the cathedrals, publish nothing, and let the fire have the headlines.
team.bestowal.definingEvent: The organ played at full stops to an empty press room: the cathedrals held, unnoticed.
team.bestowal.carryForward: The steel hammers are re-armed and unexamined, which is exactly how the cathedral prefers it.

team.blanche.summary: The intruder spent the boiling year doing the most frightening thing a white car can do: banking. While the giants burned fuel on each other, J. Herbin stacked quiet points with metronome regularity, P. White's belief now practically empiricism, and the BLANCHE 582's spotless ledger grew in the noise. The DELTA 103 V10's honesty was made for a season of attrition-by-headline: nobody remembered to defend against the polite. The white car was now standard equipment in the establishment's mirrors. The Tyrant feud ran warm underneath it all, dents answered with paperwork, and the paperwork, this year, was immaculate.
team.blanche.objective: Bank in the noise and make the white car standard equipment in every grandee's mirror.
team.blanche.definingEvent: The quiet ledger of the boiling year: points stacked while the giants burned.
team.blanche.carryForward: The establishment now defends against the polite, and the whitecoats consider that progress.

team.tyrant.summary: Japan was the duel's furnace, and the boot camp refused to be a bystander at its own race. M. Hamano carried the home round on crossover ice, the #11 in the argument while the titans traded the front row and the tannoy ran out of superlatives. G. Gould spent the fastest Sundays doing what he does: all, or the Armco, the TYRANT 548's brakes holding dares that grew longer every round. The LIZZIE 24 V8 ran hard below the fire. The walls, the crew noted, were being approached faster than ever. Nobody below decks said what everyone was thinking.
team.tyrant.objective: Refuse to be a bystander at the furnace and keep the dares longer than anyone's nerve.
team.tyrant.definingEvent: Japan: the home round held in the titans' heat, the #11 refusing the scenery.
team.tyrant.carryForward: The walls are being approached faster than ever, and the creed says approach them faster still.

team.losel.summary: The data door spent the boiling year pricing thunder. The works-versus-customer delta had never been worth more lap time, and the wind-tunnel house measured the insurgent's borrowed power to the tenth, E. Pacheco driving the sums, W. Dehehe's needle steady, the LOSEL 125's own VAPOR DNPQ V8 the honest baseline every graph quietly thanked. The model's autumn conclusion was uncomfortable reading: terminal speeds were outgrowing the circuits faster than the rulebook was growing at all. The report was bound, filed, and shown to exactly one old man in green, who read it, nodded once, and asked for a copy.
team.losel.objective: Price the works thunder to the tenth and log what the speeds are doing to the circuits.
team.losel.definingEvent: The delta file: the exact cost of establishment power, bound and quietly shared.
team.losel.carryForward: The model says the circuits are losing, and one old man in green has a copy of the sums.

team.may.summary: The little green car watched the decade's best fight from the cheap seats and kept climbing. The works war priced engines further out of reach, and the MAY 555 ran the boiling year on the LORRY 32 V8's unburstable patience, G. Turner waving from the midfield's honest rung while the titans burned above. The banner still flew for the little teams, though the little teams were learning a hard lesson: when the rich go to war, the poor pay for the fuel. Spring's ledger balanced anyway: belief, a full season, no grey hairs worth mentioning.
team.may.objective: Keep the banner flying and the green car climbing while the titans burn the sky.
team.may.definingEvent: The steady climb through the boiling year: belief, sixteen rounds, no grey hairs.
team.may.carryForward: The poor pay for the rich men's fuel, and the spring files the bill under lessons.

team.bullets.summary: The works year arrived like an answer to a decade of questions. With establishment thunder bolted into the featherweight bones, G. Ceara spent the season at the front of everything: Brazil a war drum, Japan a boil, Belgium's rain a second kingdom. The BULLETS 560 ran light, angry, and armed at last. The sister-chassis feud crossed its line at Australia, and the tribunal found the provocation as layered as the grudge. Then winter came, the pressure mounted, and the deal dissolved. Ceara shook the engineers' hands and went back to building his own thunder.
team.bullets.objective: Spend the one works season like a man who knows the deal dies in December.
team.bullets.definingEvent: Brazil: the war-drum home round, the works-powered #17 in front of the crowd that funded it.
team.bullets.carryForward: The thunder is handed back, the hands are shaken, and the lock-up starts building its own again.

team.dardan.summary: The line was crossed at Australia, and the clan paid for it in front of everyone. The sister-chassis grudge, fed for years on headlines and silence, finally boiled past sport, and the circus convened its first sporting-conduct tribunal. E. Bellini left the hearing unrepentant and diminished at once, the oxblood pride intact and the oxblood account overdrawn. K. Alfven banked what the fury spared, the DARDAN 700's VAPOR DNPQ V8 giving its documented honesty to a record now read by lawyers. The clan's whole history had been protest as warfare. Warfare, it turned out, had rules.
team.dardan.objective: Collect the debt in person and survive the reckoning the collection brings.
team.dardan.definingEvent: Australia: the line crossed, the first sporting-conduct tribunal convened.
team.dardan.carryForward: Unrepentant and diminished: the spear is checked at the door now, and the clan broods on it.

team.linden.summary: The hearing room did not entirely empty when the hearing closed, and the oldest man in it was why. M. Moreau sat through the Australia tribunal as the drivers' senior witness, and when the room rose to leave, enough stayed seated to make a habit of it. The LINDEN LN198 finished as always on the LORRY 32 V8's seasoned patience, but the tree's real year was spent after hours: counting frights, gathering signatures, asking the question with a petitioner's discipline. The drawer with the wing draft was open now, and the elder had reading light.
team.linden.objective: Keep the room seated after the hearing and turn frights into signatures.
team.linden.definingEvent: The room that stayed seated: the tribunal's aftermath, the elder's quiet congregation.
team.linden.carryForward: A congregation, a reading light, and a question that now has its own table.

team.minarae.summary: The canyons held their own audience while the titans held the headlines. B. Miller went brake-late through the boiling year with the usual canyon craft, the MINARAE 594 defending against machinery that cost more than the whole street, and J. Nono kept the promise louder than the shelf. The SEGA SG1000 V8's honest lessons looked better every round the works thunder detonated elsewhere: someone on the grid had to teach survival. The scouts' notebooks filled with the year's new requirement, drivers who could race in company at terminal speed, and the on-ramp's wares had never quoted higher.
team.minarae.objective: Hold the canyons, teach survival at terminal speed, and keep the prices honest.
team.minarae.definingEvent: The canyon classroom in the fastest year: the plain car, still teaching.
team.minarae.carryForward: The market wants drivers who can race in company, and the forge makes exactly those.

team.rigel.summary: The museum had seen duels before, and said so, gently, all season. The king and the insurgent burned through their boiling year while the RIGEL 3000 circulated below on the LORRY 32 V8's superstitious patience, R. Cotman faithful, T. Chardin courtly, the lifers telling the old stories over the good bottle: the temple years, the protest years, the wet year, the smoke. One festival was held, a finish in company that got the ledger out and the calligraphy brush cleaned. The fire next door was magnificent, the museum admitted. Fires pass. The star, and the museum's whole point, remains.
team.rigel.objective: Circulate below the fire, tell the old stories, and hold one festival.
team.rigel.definingEvent: The old duels retold over the good bottle while the new one burned.
team.rigel.carryForward: Fires pass, the museum notes, and the star is insured against exactly this.

team.comet.summary: The fastest season in history suited the light eager car right up until the bill arrived, as it always does. E. Tornio said nothing and drove everything, the COMET 323 posting the fastest flyer in its history, two laps that made the timing screens gasp before the LIZZIE 24 V8's account came due in the usual plume. The spares shelf ran on fumes, the schedule held, and the quarrel with Cool now had a speed-trap appendix. The romantics watched the tunnel duel like everyone else: hoping light and angry could still beat rich and careful.
team.comet.objective: Post the fastest flyer in the garage's history and pay the bill without complaint.
team.comet.definingEvent: The fastest flyer the garage has ever posted, two laps of pure old light.
team.comet.carryForward: The reel has its brightest frame, and the schedule now budgets for terminal speed.

team.orchis.summary: The hottest year of the horsepower war was cruel to the glasshouse, and the glasshouse bloomed anyway, once, on the afternoon the boil broke. C. Tegner read the season's single cool window like scripture, and the ORCHIS 056 opened through it into points while the titans sweated in their armour. The MISFIRE 50 V8 sulked through the scorched afternoons with the regularity of a law, and the greenhouse's ledgers recorded the arrangement with botanical patience: heat is the enemy, weather is the ally, and there is exactly one ally left. The frost gambler spent autumn praying for a colder calendar.
team.orchis.objective: Find the season's one cool window and bloom through it before the boil returns.
team.orchis.definingEvent: The afternoon the boil broke: the flower open while the titans sweated.
team.orchis.carryForward: The greenhouse prays for cold, and the prayers have started to sound like forecasts.

team.zeroforce.summary: The floor's oldest ally, attrition, arrived in the fastest year wearing terminal speed. The ZEROFORCE 231 circulated on the tired LORRY 32 V8, P. Klinger patient because the alternative costs more, and when the brave machinery wrote itself off at the new velocities, the battered car inherited places the stopwatch had never heard of. Not last, more than once, in the fastest field the circus had ever assembled: the chalk wall recorded it as a golden age. The tribunal was watched with professional interest: the floor has been on trial since it arrived, and notes standards are improving.
team.zeroforce.objective: Inherit what terminal speed drops and record the golden age in chalk.
team.zeroforce.definingEvent: Not last, more than once, in the fastest field ever assembled.
team.zeroforce.carryForward: The chalk wall calls it a golden age, and the crew's standards joke improves yearly.

team.joke.summary: The carnival read the tribunal's minutes like a review of someone else's act, and applauded the precedent with its fingers crossed. While the king and the insurgent played the main event, L. Dufay ran the between-acts show: sandbagged Saturdays, buried throttles, the JOKE 777's POND V8 mugging the midfield while the grandstand watched the fire. The sporting-conduct hearing was noted with care in the greasepaint garage: the sport had invented a law for crossing lines, and the #16's career was a study in approaching them. The act did not change. The act never changes. The lighting did.
team.joke.objective: Run the between-acts show and study the new law's exact edges.
team.joke.definingEvent: The tribunal's minutes, read in the greasepaint garage with unusual care.
team.joke.carryForward: The sport has a line now, and the clown has measured it with a tailor's eye.

team.lares.summary: The record held through the hottest summer, and the hearth called that the season. P. Arai's gentleman's patience brought the stout LARES 92 home through the boiling year lap after lap, the RAM V12's geological beat unmoved, while the duel's debris and the season's frights wrote off quicker machinery around him. Every fright walked away from was toasted with relief, not excitement. The lamp stayed lit, the door stayed open, and the finishing record grew another honest ring. The paddock had two spectacles this year: the fire, and the thing that never caught it.
team.lares.objective: Hold the record through the hottest summer and toast every walk-away.
team.lares.definingEvent: The record unbroken through the boiling year: another honest ring.
team.lares.carryForward: The thing that never catches fire is becoming the paddock's favourite superstition.

team.feet.summary: The boil was just overtime at a higher temperature, and the grafters punched in as usual. J. Rampal dragged Old Boots through the fastest season in history with the same clockwork fury, the FEET 13's hand-fettled YOUGEN V10 finishing while the works thunder detonated around it. The working men watched the tunnel duel the way miners watch fireworks: professionally, from a distance, with opinions about the wiring. One Sunday the battered blue car came home ahead of a machine whose spark plugs cost more than the garage, and the pit lane's mechanics nodded the nod that means the ledger noticed.
team.feet.objective: Punch in, outlast the boil, and keep the sweat invoice payable.
team.feet.definingEvent: The Sunday Old Boots outlasted a machine whose plugs cost more than the garage.
team.feet.carryForward: The nod was given again, and the grafters' ledger is the paddock's worst-kept secret.

team.serga.summary: The thousand small names rode through the boiling year on the grey car's flanks, every one of them finishing. E. Sambena welded, drove, and signed his own cheques through the fastest season yet staged, the SERGA 1000 filling mirrors on the afternoons the titans were not using, the SC3000 F12 eager once warmed, opinionated about its oil. Last summer's debt was being repaid the only way the mountain man knows: in finishes. The duel was watched with a craftsman's envy and a welder's verdict: lovely work, shame about the budget. The gate still opens for whoever welds hard enough.
team.serga.objective: Repay the thousand names in finishes and keep the gate welded open.
team.serga.definingEvent: The names carried through the boiling year, every one of them finishing.
team.serga.carryForward: The debt shrinks by the finish, and the welder's verdict stands: lovely work, shame about the budget.

team.cool.summary: The deckchairs had the finest view of the decade's best show, and the calmest garage in the circus dressed for it. A. Delvaux composed love letters to the hairpins between the main events, the COOL 05's CORSE V8 sulking on schedule and running sweetly off it, and when the tunnel duel ran nose-to-tail through the dark, the cheap end toasted it like a title fight. The gold war, the works thunder, the tribunal: all excellent theatre, all somebody else's. The garage's own season was a point, a sunset, and a hat passed for a neighbour.
team.cool.objective: Dress for the main event, toast the duel, and keep the sunsets coming.
team.cool.definingEvent: The tunnel duel, toasted from the deckchairs like a title fight.
team.cool.carryForward: The decade's best show is over, and the deckchairs intend to renew their subscription.

team.moon.summary: The back row, it turns out, is the finest seat for history. K. Yepes waved at the duel as it lapped him, and the MOON 292 circled the boiling year with a body's patience, the RAM V12's overbuilt beat outlasting the works thunder's temper. The logbook's line: present at every flag of the fire. The far side watched the tribunal with the distant interest of a body never once accused of anything. The gap to the pack shrank by its annual degree. The telescope stayed pointed up. The fire was magnificent, and the orbit was unmoved.
team.moon.objective: Witness the fire from the finest seat and keep the orbit unmoved.
team.moon.definingEvent: Present at every flag of the fire, the logbook's quietest line.
team.moon.carryForward: The orbit rises by its annual degree, and history keeps lapping the telescope.

team.iris.summary: The violet flame refused to let the season become a two-man story, and made third wheel an honour instead of a consolation. B. Salgado carved between the titans all year, the IRIS 717 arriving in the duel's arguments with jeweller's timing, the PRISM 90 V10 singing its clean band in the gaps the war drums left. The white gloves drove the year like a campaign between emperors: when the fire spent itself, the jewel would be standing. The treasury grew; the crown stayed; the hunger was now a programme with a podium's worth of patience.
team.iris.objective: Claim third wheel as an honour and be standing when the fire spends itself.
team.iris.definingEvent: The violet car carved between the titans: third wheel, claimed as an honour.
team.iris.carryForward: The programme has patience, the hunger has a podium, and the fire will tire.

team.azalea.summary: The frost collected the afternoons the duel left unattended. While the grandstands drummed for the king and the insurgent, M. Larssen read the boiling year for its forgotten decisive hours, and the AZALEA 808 opened over them with glacial punctuality, the BLOOM 88 V8 building from held breath to full song on exactly the days the fire forgot to win. The sister war kept its pattern: Iris loud between the titans, Azalea silent over the dates. The cabinet gained entries the grid could name. The frost does not duel; the frost harvests, and the boiling year was a harvest.
team.azalea.objective: Harvest the decisive hours the duel forgets and let the fire have the noise.
team.azalea.definingEvent: The afternoons the fire forgot to win, opened over by the bloom.
team.azalea.carryForward: The family ledger: the loud sister joins the argument, the cold one owns the dates.
